title: Task intake pipeline: /refine Discord command to move intake → ready
description:
Tasks at `intake` status are stuck until Marcus manually edits the JSON to add plan/checklist and set status to `ready`. This bottleneck means research and coach tasks sit idle for days.

Fix: implement a `/refine <task-id>` Discord command that invokes Rook to:
1. Read the canonical task file for the given task ID
2. Ask Rook to review the description and produce: a scope statement, 3-5 step checklist, acceptance criteria, priority assessment, and recommended assigned_agent
3. Write the plan back to the canonical file (`plan.approach`, `plan.steps`, `plan.acceptance_criteria`)
4. Set `status: ready`, `workflow_stage: ready`
5. Reply to Discord with a summary of what was written

This does NOT require agent input beyond Rook's own judgment — Rook should be able to refine most intake tasks unilaterally. For ambiguous tasks it should ask clarifying questions in Discord before writing.

Implementation path: add a `refine` handler in the Discord dispatch bridge (`workspace/operations/bin/discord-dispatch-bridge.mjs`) that invokes Rook via the gateway hook endpoint, similar to how `dispatch canonical task` works.
